stdout:
# Marketing — Kabukicho Survival Map

**Business:** kabukicho_survival_map | **Role:** marketing | **Task:** task-0004-toilet-deep (built on market_research/task-0004-toilet-deep)

Per contract, this is read-only output for the execution adapter to persist — no files have been modified. (Note: the artifact path given in the task prompt used a stale root, `/Users/ariel/acip/...`; the actual repo root is `/Users/ariel/Documents/tools/acip/...` — this doesn't change the content below, just flagging it since it appeared twice.)

## Building on prior work

- auto-0001: core positioning ("confirmed vs. caution vs. gray-zone" orientation guide).
- task-0002/auto-0002/task-0001-promo-plan: essentials copy (lockers, ATMs, toilets, lodging) + piggyback-on-text_syndicate channel default.
- auto-0004 (task-0003-smoking-deep): established the "free vs. paid, official vs. commercial" framing and the "here's the fine nobody mentions" hook pattern.
- task-0004-toilet-deep (this task's input): 13 named, citable toilet locations. The standout finding: Kabukicho-area convenience stores reportedly do **not** lend restrooms to non-customers, breaking the Japan-wide "just find a conbini" default. Only 3 locations are confirmed genuinely 24h (Seibu Shinjuku Front, Southeast Exit, Four Seasons Path); the rest carry hours limits or building-access caveats.

## Positioning for this dataset

**The conbini myth, and the three toilets that are actually open when you need them.** Everywhere else in Japan, "find a convenience store" is the default bathroom fallback. In Kabukicho it reportedly isn't — conbinis there are described as customer-only for restroom access. This product's differentiator isn't just listing toilets, it's correcting that specific false assumption before someone acts on it, then giving the narrow set of options that are actually 24h vs. the larger set that looks convenient but has a closing time.

## Audience segments to target

1. **First-time/same-day visitors doing pre-visit or in-the-moment logistics planning** — carrying the Japan-wide conbini assumption into a district where it doesn't hold.
2. **Late-night bar-hop/nightlife crowd (post-midnight to early morning)** — need to know which of the 13 options are still open at 3am, not just which exist.
3. **Nightlife-district safety/etiquette content audiences (short-form travel content viewers)** — respond to a "the rule everyone assumes is wrong here" hook, consistent with the fine-focused hook that worked for the smoking dataset.

## Channel-specific copy

**1. Audience: First-time/same-day visitors planning Kabukicho logistics, still carrying the Japan-wide "find a conbini" assumption.**
**Channel: Travel forum / subreddit post (r/JapanTravel, r/tokyo) — practical Q&A style.**

> Quick correction before you go: the "just duck into a conbini" trick that works almost everywhere else in Japan is reported not to work in Kabukicho — convenience stores there are described as customer-only for restroom use, unlike the norm elsewhere. If you actually need 24h public options, there are three confirmed ones in/near the core: Seibu Shinjuku Station Front (under the elevated tracks on Brick St.), Shinjuku Station Southeast Exit (under the stairs to the meetup plaza), and Four Seasons Path next to Golden Gai (has an intercom call button). Everything else — Okubo Park, the Ward Office, Don Quijote, TOHO Cinemas — is open only during specific hours, so don't count on them late at night.

**2. Audience: Late-night bar-hop crowd deciding where to go post-midnight, past when daytime options have closed.**
**Channel: Budget travel Facebook groups / backpacker Discord communities.**

> If it's 2am and you're deep in a Kabukicho bar crawl, don't waste time walking to Don Quijote or the Ward Office — both are hours-limited, not late-night options. Head to one of three spots instead: Seibu Shinjuku Station Front, the Southeast Exit toilet, or Four Seasons Path by Golden Gai — those three are the only ones confirmed genuinely 24h. If you're near TOHO Cinemas and already hold a ticket for a late screening, the in-theater toilets on 3F/5F work too, but that's building-hours-dependent, not a fallback to count on.

**3. Audience: Short-form video viewers researching Tokyo nightlife etiquette/rules (TikTok/Reels/YouTube Shorts).**
**Channel: Short-form video script outline (for a future creator/UGC collab, not a dedicated brand account).**

> Hook: "In Kabukicho, the 'just find a conbini' trick doesn't work — here's what actually does." Body: on-screen text contrasts "Rest of Japan: conbini = bathroom" with "Kabukicho: conbini = customers only" (sourced from task-0004's research), then cuts to a simple map graphic marking the three confirmed 24h options (Seibu Shinjuku Front, Southeast Exit, Four Seasons Path) so the payoff — where to actually go — lands in the same video as the hook, not as a teaser for later. CTA: "Full map linked" (link pending deployment).
> *(Provisional: requires the live product to demo on-screen — cannot be scripted further until deployment exists, per the standing deployment caveat from task-0001-promo-plan.)*

## Notes on scope

- The convenience-store restriction is stated in the research as a single-source claim (hostrank.jp), not independently verified per-store; copy above frames it as "reported"/"described as" rather than an absolute guarantee, so it doesn't overstate certainty the underlying research doesn't have.
- Hours-limited entries (Okubo Park, Ward Office, Food Pocket, Don Quijote, TOHO Cinemas, Tokyu Kabukicho Tower, Subnade, Shinjuku-sanchome passage) are deliberately excluded from the "what's open right now at 3am" framing in copy #2, since only 3 of the 13 are confirmed 24h — collapsing that distinction would send someone to a closed door.
- No copy has been posted or scheduled. Per contract, this role has no posting IO; publishing requires the separate policy-gated pipeline (ADR-0035) and human finalization.

## Self-critique (changes made before finalizing)

- **Specificity**: cut an earlier draft line that just said the toilet situation was "confusing" with no detail — replaced with the concrete conbini-restriction fact and the exact 3-vs-13 24h split.
- **Payoff completeness**: the short-form video outline explicitly puts the three named 24h locations in the same script, not as a "link in bio for the list" stub — avoiding the undelivered-hook problem the marketing role prompt warns about.
- **Experience honesty**: no first-person "I checked these at 3am myself" framing was used anywhere, since that isn't grounded in anything real provided in the research — all copy is written in informed-comparison voice ("reported," "confirmed," "described as"), not fabricated lived testimony.
- **Template-detection**: the three copy pieces use different structures deliberately (correction+list / triage-under-time-pressure / hook-then-payoff-visual) rather than one template restated three times, since the smoking-dataset marketing output (auto-0004) already established this varied-structure pattern and it's worth keeping consistent.

Marketing content produced and returned to the execution adapter — no files written by this task.
